peeel.name: PEEEL (Single Idea Paragraph)
peeel.description: One main idea, developed fully through the paragraph.
peeel.when_to_use: Any essay type. Safest and most versatile structure.

slots.name: Point
slots.function: State the main idea of the paragraph
slots.description: A clear claim or topic sentence. Usually contains a noun phrase that names the issue.
slots.vocab_profile: Abstract nouns, opinion verbs, topic-specific nouns

slots.name: Explain
slots.function: Answer HOW or WHY the point is true
slots.description: The mechanism or reasoning behind the claim. Explains the logic.
slots.vocab_profile: Causation verbs (leads to, stems from, results in), because/since clauses

slots.name: Example
slots.function: Provide evidence or a specific instance
slots.description: A concrete example, statistic, case study, or real-world reference.
slots.vocab_profile: For instance, such as, for example, naming specific places/studies

slots.name: Effect
slots.function: Show the broader impact — answer SO WHAT? or IF NOT?
slots.description: Takes the idea as far as it can go. What is the wider consequence? Or: what would opponents say, and why are they wrong?
slots.vocab_profile: Consequently, as a result, this means that, without this

slots.name: Link
slots.function: Connect back to the essay topic or thesis
slots.description: A concluding sentence that echoes the thesis or bridges to the next paragraph.
slots.vocab_profile: Therefore, clearly, it is evident that, this demonstrates

upepe.name: UPEPE (Double Idea Paragraph)
upepe.description: Two main points covered in one paragraph, introduced by an umbrella sentence.
upepe.when_to_use: When you have two related reasons, causes, or solutions. Common in causes/solutions and advantages/disadvantages essays.

slots.name: Umbrella
slots.function: Introduce both points with a covering sentence
slots.description: A topic sentence that signals two ideas are coming. E.g., 'There are two main reasons for this.'
slots.vocab_profile: Two main, several, a number of, key factors

slots.name: Point 1
slots.description: Introduce the first reason, cause, or advantage.
slots.vocab_profile: The first is, one major factor, the primary reason

slots.name: Extend 1
slots.function: Develop the first point with explanation or example
slots.description: Support Point 1 with reasoning, an example, or evidence.

slots.name: Point 2
slots.description: Introduce the second reason, cause, or advantage.
slots.vocab_profile: The other factor, in addition to this, another issue

slots.name: Extend 2
slots.function: Develop the second point with explanation or example
slots.description: Support Point 2 with reasoning, an example, or evidence.

task1.B8: was almost double that of / exceeded X by a considerable margin
was significantly lower, at roughly half the level of
reached a peak of / hit its highest point at
fell to its lowest point of / reached a low of

task2.B8: Despite this / In spite of this

task2.B7: Indeed, X is a case in point
task2.B8: A clear example of this can be seen in

task2.B7: Although it could be argued that
task2.B8: Admittedly, there is some merit in the view that

task2.B7: In conclusion, while X, I believe Y
task2.B8: To conclude, although X has merit, the evidence suggests Y

task2.B6: There are several reasons for this
task2.B7: A number of factors contribute to this
task2.B8: This phenomenon can be attributed to several interrelated factors

task2.B6: The main reason / The other factor
task2.B7: One key factor / Another contributing element
task2.B8: The primary driver / Equally significant is
